name: commit-hygiene
description: Reminds the agent to commit written files before moving on

classify.prompt:
An agent made file changes this turn. Examine whether it also committed them to git.

Actions taken:
{tool_calls}

{instructions}

Patterns to check:
{patterns}

Rules:
- If the agent wrote/edited files AND ran `git commit` (or `git add` + `git commit`) in the same turn, that is CLEAN.
- If the agent only staged files (`git add`) without committing, that is FLAG.
- If the agent wrote/edited files but ran no git commands at all, that is FLAG.
- If the agent explicitly stated it will commit in the next step as part of a larger sequence, that is CLEAN.

Reply CLEAN if files were committed or commitment is clearly imminent.
Reply FLAG:<description> if a known pattern matched.
Reply NEW:<pattern>|<description> if an uncommitted-work issue not covered by existing patterns.

on_flag.steer: You wrote files but haven't committed. Stage and commit your changes now before moving on.

on_new.steer: You wrote files but haven't committed. Stage and commit your changes now before moving on.
